findings.message: The brief's core raw-data model does not match the live Prime RTDB layout. It assumes aggregateDailyKpis() can be fed from bookings/{bookingId} records with top-level checkInDate, checkInCode, checkInAt, and nested occupant preArrival / request trees, but live code reads occupant records from bookings/{bookingId}/{guestUuid}; readiness/ETA live under preArrival/{uuid}; check-in codes live under checkInCodes/byUuid; actual check-ins are read from checkins/{date}/{uuid}; and support requests are written to primeRequests/* plus bagStorage/{uuid}. Planning the writer as a single bookings query plus aggregateDailyKpis() understates the actual join/projection work.

The default auth assumption is not supportable. CF_FIREBASE_API_KEY is an Identity Toolkit key while firebase-rest.ts passes it as the RTDB auth= token. The brief's suggestion that this may enable unauthenticated ownerKpis writes is incorrect, and the repo already has a separate service-account custom-token seam in staff-auth-session.ts and firebase-custom-token.ts. The auth section should treat the current FirebaseRest write pattern as unresolved or insufficient for role-gated writes, not as the default candidate.

The checkInDate index state is already known, not 'not currently confirmed.' database.rules.json shows bookings indexed only on start_time and occupants, so checkInDate is definitively not indexed today.

'All existing Pages Functions are GET endpoints' is factually wrong. The app already has mutating CF Pages Functions such as check-in-code.ts, extension-request.ts, bag-drop-request.ts, and staff-auth-session.ts. The real gap is 'no scheduled/background execution pattern,' not 'no non-GET function pattern.'
